frameworks.name: subjective economic valuation
frameworks.one_line: Momentary willingness-to-pay as instantaneous subjective value

frameworks.name: multiplicative gain on value
frameworks.one_line: Craving rescales economic value by a selective gain factor

frameworks.name: attribute-based similarity generalization
frameworks.one_line: Craving generalizes across goods in proportion to attribute similarity

frameworks.name: cue-induced craving
frameworks.one_line: Selective cue exposure as the laboratory induction of desire state

core_question: How does a craving-like desire state computationally transform subjective valuation to bias choice?
key_assumptions: Goods can be embedded in a subjective attribute similarity space
Craving acts on valuation rather than on choice noise
manipulations: Selective cue exposure to a specific high-calorie snack
Attribute similarity between exposed and nonexposed goods
key_findings: Cue exposure raised willingness-to-pay for the exposed snack
The effect generalized to nonexposed goods proportional to subjective attribute similarity
Effects of reward size scaled consistent with a multiplicative gain on value
interpretations: Craving is a selective multiplicative gain on economic value rather than an additive bias
Generalization tracks subjective similarity, defining the selectivity of the gain
